title: Constantine — to the Council of Nicaea

intro.title: The Council and the City
intro.body: After defeating Licinius at Chrysopolis in September 324, Constantine reunited the empire and convened the first ecumenical council at Nicaea in May 325 to resolve the Arian controversy. The same year he began laying out his new capital across the Bosphorus.

stops.name: Thessalonica
stops.note: Constantine's residence during the Licinius war. Galerius's earlier rotunda and arch dominate the city.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Macedonian port with city walls, Galerian rotunda dome, fleet at anchor.

stops.name: Chrysopolis
stops.note: Constantine's decisive victory over Licinius, 18 September 324, on the Asian shore opposite Byzantium. Modern Üsküdar.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Battle on a coastal slope, Bosphorus visible, labarum standard at the head of a column.

stops.name: Byzantium / Constantinople
stops.note: Refoundation begun 8 November 324. The new walls trace a vast peninsula; consecration follows in 330.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Surveyors with the emperor on a hilltop, Golden Horn below, traced wall-lines striking inland.

stops.name: Nicaea
stops.note: Imperial summer palace on Lake Ascanius. Site of the council, 20 May - 19 June 325. ~318 bishops attended.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lake-side basilica with rows of seated bishops, emperor enthroned on a low dais.

stops.name: Antioch
stops.note: Constantine winters at Antioch after Nicaea, then turns east toward the Persian frontier in the late 320s.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Orontes city in evening, lamps in the colonnaded street, imperial party at the river palace.
